---
title: "Chapter 4.2: Outcome-Blind Simulation Studies"
format: html
---

*This chapter is a placeholder and will be filled in with content on designing and running outcome-blind simulation studies to evaluate estimator performance.*

## Planned content

- Why simulate before analyzing real data
- Designing a data-generating process that reflects the target trial
- Evaluating bias, variance, coverage, and MSE of candidate estimators
- Using simulations to diagnose positivity violations and model misspecification
- Outcome-blind protocol: locking the analysis plan before unblinding outcomes
- Worked example using `tlverse` and `simcausal`

## Sources and further reading

- Petersen ML, van der Laan MJ (2014). Causal models and learning from data. *Epidemiology* 25(3):418-426.
- Dang LE, Tager I, Petersen ML (2023). A causal roadmap for generating high-quality real-world evidence. *Journal of Clinical and Translational Science* 7(1):e127.
- Morris TP, White IR, Crowther MJ (2019). Using simulation studies to evaluate statistical methods. *Statistics in Medicine* 38(11):2074-2102.
